title: Local variables should not be declared and then immediately returned or thrown
description: Declaring a variable only to immediately return or throw it is considered a bad practice because it adds unnecessary complexity to the code. This practice can make the code harder to read and understand, as it introduces an extra step that doesn't add any value. Instead of declaring a variable and then immediately returning or throwing it, it is generally better to return or throw the value directly.
recommendation: Remove the unnecessary variable declaration and return/throw the value directly.

noncompliant: function test() { const result = calculate(); return result; }
function test() { let error = new Error('message'); throw error; }

checks.message: Local variable is declared and then immediately returned or thrown - consider returning/throwing the value directly
